title: Fix post-login routing: skip character creation when character already exists

requirements.text: In App.tsx, after a user logs in and a realm is selected, query the backend for an existing character for that realm before showing any screen. If a character already exists for the selected realm in the current season, skip the CharacterCreationScreen entirely and route the player directly to the character sheet / dungeon select screen. The character creation screen must only be shown when no character exists for the chosen realm.

quotes: Before showing the character creation screen, check if a character already exists for the selected realm — if so, skip creation and route directly to the character sheet/dungeon screen.
if a character already exists for the selected realm (Softcore or Hardcore), the app skips character creation entirely and routes the player directly to the character sheet/dungeon screen

acceptanceCriteria: After login, if a character exists for the selected realm, the CharacterCreationScreen is never rendered and the player lands on the character sheet or dungeon select screen.
After login, if no character exists for the selected realm, the CharacterCreationScreen is shown as normal.
This behavior applies to both Softcore and Hardcore realm selections.
No 'Actor Not Available' or unhandled error is shown when a character already exists.

requirements.text: In the backend (main.mo), ensure the createCharacter function returns a clear #alreadyExists error variant when a character already exists for the calling principal in the selected realm and current season. The frontend must never receive an ambiguous error for this case.

quotes: Ensure createCharacter returns a clear #alreadyExists variant so the frontend can distinguish 'already has a character' from a genuine failure.

acceptanceCriteria: Calling createCharacter when a character already exists returns a Result with an #alreadyExists error variant.
The #alreadyExists variant is distinct from other error variants (e.g., #invalidInput, #unauthorized).
Calling createCharacter when no character exists for that realm proceeds normally and creates the character.

requirements.text: In App.tsx, if createCharacter is called and returns an #alreadyExists error (e.g., due to a race condition or duplicate call), the frontend must silently redirect the player to the character sheet / dungeon select screen rather than displaying an error message or crashing.

quotes: If createCharacter is somehow still called and returns #alreadyExists, the frontend should silently redirect rather than showing an error.

acceptanceCriteria: If createCharacter returns #alreadyExists, no error toast or error screen is shown.
The player is automatically routed to the character sheet or dungeon select screen.
The app does not crash or enter an unrecoverable state.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor file (backend/main.mo).
nonGoals: Changing the character creation form UI or stat allocation flow.
Modifying realm selection logic beyond the routing fix.
Any changes to combat, loot, or leaderboard systems.
